regime: Risk-On Reclaim / Whipsaw (Equities Flip Long Post-FOMC Minutes)
summary: One session after flipping short, equities snapped back. MNQ, MES and MGC all washed to fresh overnight lows (MNQ 28,910, MES 7,468, MGC 4,062) then recovered through the FOMC June minutes and flipped their 1hr stops back long by the close. It reads as a squeeze of yesterday's reversal shorts, not a confirmed trend: NQ's stop has now flipped three sessions running (long, short, long). Crude was the one clean move, +3.55% for a second straight up day. The book stays flat and DEFENSIVE; chop, not trend, is the operating assumption.

keyLevels.label: MNQ ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Flipped LONG; price +335 above. The line for the fresh long.

keyLevels.label: MNQ AVWAP / OR High
keyLevels.note: Closed above both; a 2-close hold confirms, a loss reopens the chop.

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop / RTH Low
keyLevels.note: Flipped long; 7,468 O/N low is the pivot, 7,602 record above.

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Flipped long but late; +48 above, below the 4,184 prior stop.

keyLevels.label: MCL ATR Stop / psych
keyLevels.note: Rising LONG; +1.17 above after a +8.7% two-day surge.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (long)
setups.entry: Long continuation only on a 2-close hold above the 29,386 OR high; not at the highs
setups.bias: BORDERLINE long. Whipsaw tape (3 flips in 3 sessions); reduced size, demand confirmation.

setups.name: AVWAP Bounce (MNQ / MES)
setups.entry: Long on a hold of the session AVWAP (MNQ 29,323, MES 7,512) as support
setups.t1: OR high, then the O/N high
setups.bias: BORDERLINE. AVWAP flipped to support on the reclaim; a loss reopens the chop.

event.name: FOMC June-meeting minutes (released 14:00 ET)
event.actual: Equities entered soft and washed to fresh overnight lows (MNQ 28,910, MES 7,468, MGC 4,062), then recovered through the 14:00 ET release and flipped their 1hr stops back long by the close.
event.nqReaction: MNQ dropped through its 29,201 OR low and the 29,000 psych to 28,910, then V-reclaimed to close 29,390 above the OR high, flipping its stop long at 29,055 (its 3rd flip in 3 sessions).
event.crossAsset: Crude led again (+3.55%, 2nd up day, +8.7% two-day). Equities and gold flipped long via stop ratchets on the reclaim but closed red. The snapback squeezed the reversal shorts rather than starting a fresh trend.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: The two-way range left an unstable close; watch Thursday's open, no clean fade yet.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer-horizon base held the 28,910 washout and the short-horizon flip re-aligned long.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES flipped long with NQ, but both closed red and the alignment came from a stop ratchet, not strength, so it is fragile.

risk.throttleState: DEFENSIVE. Signals flipped long, but three stop flips in three sessions is chop, and chop is where trend edges bleed.
risk.ddProximity: Not binding; the book entered flat and the full daily budget is intact.
risk.sizing: A TSMOM-20 long is a starter only, on a 2-close hold above the 29,055 stop and the 29,386 OR high, not at the highs. The gold long is late and the crude long chases a +8.7% two-day move, so neither is a fresh entry.
risk.note: Week ahead: CPI (June) next week. Thursday is a normal session; treat the whipsaw as the base case and demand 2-close confirmation before adding size.

psychology: Three flips in three sessions is the market telling you what it is: chop, not trend. After being whipsawed short yesterday, the pull is to trust today's long flip at full size and make it back. That instinct is what a whipsaw feeds on. Demand a 2-close hold, size down, and let the tape prove it can trend before you press. A stop that flips every session is a signal to trade smaller, not harder.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidenceNote
DirectionRISK-ON RECLAIM / WHIPSAWMediumMNQ, MES and MGC all washed out overnight then reclaimed and flipped their 1hr stops LONG. NQ's 3rd flip in 3 sessions. Crude extended a 2-day surge.
VolatilityEXPANDING / HIGHHighMNQ ATR14 157.65 (from 123); MES 21.97 (from 14.7). Huge intraday range: MNQ 29,558 down to 28,910 and back.
SessionPOST-EVENTHighFOMC June minutes released today 14:00 ET; the tape recovered through and after the release.
InventoryTrapped Jul 7 shorts squeezed / momentum-long crudeMediumReversal shorts from yesterday caught on the reclaim; energy extended higher.
EventPOST_EVENT (FOMC minutes)MediumNo fresh Tier-1 print after; CPI (June) lands next week.
LiquidityNormalMediumFull depth, no holiday distortion.

fullReport.html:

2MNQ accepts (2 closes) below the 29,210 RTH low / 29,194 -3sTRIGGERED / PLAYED OUTFive straight hourly closes below 29,210; the air pocket opened through the 29,000 psych to a 28,910 low, then V-reclaimed.
3MES loses the 7,530 RTH low / 7,500 psych on hourly closesTRIGGERED / MIXEDLost both to a 7,468 low, but reclaimed 7,500 and flipped its stop long by the close (7,515.25).

5MGC 2-closes back above its 4,184 stopNOT TRIGGEREDNever reclaimed 4,184 (max close 4,168); fell to a 4,062 low then bounced but stayed below.

Score: 5 rows, 3 triggered (2 played out, 1 mixed), 0 failed, 2 not triggered. July MTD: 12 triggered / 6 played out / 0 failed.
